channel.title: AI Unfiltered
channel.description: Chinese AI • Open Source • Security • Incidents. Signal, not noise.

item.title: Preparing for a ‘vulnerability patch wave’
item.description: Organisations must act now to prepare for a wave of patches that will address decades of technical debt.

item.title: Poisoned Ruby Gems and Go Modules Exploit CI Pipelines for Credential Theft
item.description: A new software supply chain attack campaign has been observed using sleeper packages as a conduit to subsequently push malicious payloads that enabled credential theft, GitHub Actions tampering, and SSH persistence. The activity has been attributed to the GitHub account "BufferZoneCorp," which has...

item.title: Trump’s mass firing just dealt another blow to American science
item.description: This past week delivered another gut punch for science in the US. This time, the target was the National Science Foundation—a federal agency that funds major research projects to the tune of around $9 billion. The foundation’s efforts were overseen by a board of 22 prominent scientists. On Friday...

item.title: A new T-Mobile network for Christians aims to block porn and gender-related content
item.description: A new US-wide cell phone network marketed to Christians is set to launch next week. It blocks porn, which experts in network security say marks the first time a US cell plan has used network-level blocking for such content that can’t be turned off even by adult account owners. It’s also rolling out...

item.title: Hugging Face, ClawHub Abused for Malware Distribution
item.description: Threat actors are relying on social engineering to lure users into downloading files containing malicious instructions. The post Hugging Face, ClawHub Abused for Malware Distribution appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: FBI Warns of Surge in Hacker-Enabled Cargo Theft
item.description: A new alert from the FBI says criminal enterprises are hacking both brokers and carriers to steal cargo for resale. The post FBI Warns of Surge in Hacker-Enabled Cargo Theft appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: 1,800 Hit in Mini Shai-Hulud Attack on SAP, Lightning, Intercom
item.description: The compromised Lightning and Intercom packages have a combined monthly download count of nearly 10 million. The post 1,800 Hit in Mini Shai-Hulud Attack on SAP, Lightning, Intercom appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: [AINews] Agents for Everything Else: Codex for Knowledge Work, Claude for Creative Work
item.description: a quiet day lets us reflect on coding agents "breaking containment"

item.title: Elevated error rates affecting ChatGPT for some users in Europe
item.description: Status: ResolvedAll impacted services have now fully recovered.

item.title: Compositional Meta-Learning for Mitigating Task Heterogeneity in Physics-Informed Neural Networks
item.description: arXiv:2604.26999v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Physics-informed neural networks (PINNs) approximate solutions of partial differential equations (PDEs) by embedding physical laws into the loss function. In parameterized PDE families, variations in coefficients or boundary/initial conditions define...

item.title: Binary Spiking Neural Networks as Causal Models
item.description: arXiv:2604.27007v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We provide a causal analysis of Binary Spiking Neural Networks (BSNNs) to explain their behavior. We formally define a BSNN and represent its spiking activity as a binary causal model. Thanks to this causal representation, we are able to explain the...

item.title: When Your LLM Reaches End-of-Life: A Framework for Confident Model Migration in Production Systems
item.description: arXiv:2604.27082v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We present a framework for migrating production Large Language Model (LLM) based systems when the underlying model reaches end-of-life or requires replacement. The key contribution is a Bayesian statistical approach that calibrates automated...

item.title: End-to-end autonomous scientific discovery on a real optical platform
item.description: arXiv:2604.27092v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Scientific research has long been human-led, driving new knowledge and transformative technologies through the continual revision of questions, methods and claims as evidence accumulates. Although large language model (LLM)-based agents are beginning...

item.title: Think it, Run it: Autonomous ML pipeline generation via self-healing multi-agent AI
item.description: arXiv:2604.27096v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The purpose of our paper is to develop a unified multi-agent architecture that automates end-to-end machine learning (ML) pipeline generation from datasets and natural-language (NL) goals, improving efficiency, robustness and explainability. A...

item.title: Monitoring Neural Training with Topology: A Footprint-Predictable Collapse Index
item.description: arXiv:2604.26984v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Representational collapse, where embeddings become anisotropic and lose multi-scale structure, can erode downstream performance long before performance metrics react. We propose an online, topology-aware monitor for evolving neural representations...

item.title: Simple Self-Conditioning Adaptation for Masked Diffusion Models
item.description: arXiv:2604.26985v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Masked diffusion models (MDMs) generate discrete sequences by iterative denoising under an absorbing masking process. In standard masked diffusion, if a token remains masked after a reverse update, the model discards its clean-state prediction for...

item.title: People-Centred Medical Image Analysis
item.description: arXiv:2604.26991v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Recent advances in data-centric medical AI have produced highly accurate diagnostic systems, but the emphasis on data curation and performance metrics has not translated into widespread clinical adoption. We conjecture that this limited uptake stems...

item.title: When Continual Learning Moves to Memory: A Study of Experience Reuse in LLM Agents
item.description: arXiv:2604.27003v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Memory-augmented LLM agents offer an appealing shortcut to continual learning: rather than updating model parameters, they accumulate experience in external memory, seemingly sidestepping the stability-plasticity dilemma of parametric learning. We...

item.title: Automatic Causal Fairness Analysis with LLM-Generated Reporting
item.description: arXiv:2604.27011v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: AutoML, intended as the process of automating the application of machine learning to real-world problems, is a key step for AI popularisation. Most AutoML frameworks are not accounting for the potential lack of fairness in the training data and in the...

item.title: BatteryPass-12K: The First Dataset for the Novel Digital Battery Passport Conformance Task
item.description: arXiv:2604.26986v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We introduce a novel task of digital battery passport (DBP) conformance classification and introduce the first public benchmark for the task: BatteryPass-12K, created synthetically from real pilot samples. This is as the EU's battery regulation on...

item.title: Length Value Model: Scalable Value Pretraining for Token-Level Length Modeling
item.description: arXiv:2604.27039v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Token serves as the fundamental unit of computation in modern autoregressive models, and generation length directly influences both inference cost and reasoning performance. Despite its importance, existing approaches lack fine-grained length...

item.title: CL-bench Life: Can Language Models Learn from Real-Life Context?
item.description: arXiv:2604.27043v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Today's AI assistants such as OpenClaw are designed to handle context effectively, making context learning an increasingly important capability for models. As these systems move beyond professional settings into everyday life, the nature of the...

item.title: Useless but Safe? Benchmarking Utility Recovery with User Intent Clarification in Multi-Turn Conversations
item.description: arXiv:2604.27093v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Current LLM safety alignment techniques improve model robustness against adversarial attacks, but overlook whether and how LLMs can recover helpfulness when benign users clarify their intent. We introduce CarryOnBench, the first interactive benchmark...

item.title: Exploring the Limits of Pruning: Task-Specific Neurons, Model Collapse, and Recovery in Task-Specific Large Language Models
item.description: arXiv:2604.27115v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Neuron pruning is widely used to reduce the computational cost and parameter footprint of large language models, yet it remains unclear whether neurons in task-specific models contribute uniformly to task performance. In this work, we provide...

item.title: Load Balancing health monitoring impacted
item.description: May 1, 02:48 UTCResolved - This incident has been resolved.Apr 30, 22:49 UTCMonitoring - A fix has been implemented and we are monitoring the results.Apr 30, 22:31 UTCInvestigating - Cloudflare is currently investigating issues with its Load Balancing Health Monitoring services. During this time...

item.title: Analytics - Web Analytics Delays
item.description: Apr 30, 23:26 UTCResolved - This incident has been resolved.Apr 30, 23:23 UTCIdentified - The issue has been identified and a fix is being implemented.Apr 30, 23:23 UTCInvestigating - Cloudflare Web Analytics processing is running behind. This affects timely delivery of customer data. These delays...

item.title: Durable Object and D1 Analytics Delayed
item.description: Apr 30, 23:04 UTCIdentified - The storage totals shown in the dashboard for Durable Objects and D1 are currently delayed.

item.title: Anthropic Unveils Claude Security to Counter AI-Powered Exploit Surge
item.description: With Mythos signaling a new era of near-instant exploitation, Anthropic positions Claude Security to help defenders keep pace. The post Anthropic Unveils Claude Security to Counter AI-Powered Exploit Surge appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: AI Fuels ‘Industrial’ Cybercrime as Time-to-Exploit Shrinks to Hours
item.description: Industrialized cybercrime delivers attacks with greater scale, speed and success. Defenders must match this with use of AI and automation. The post AI Fuels ‘Industrial’ Cybercrime as Time-to-Exploit Shrinks to Hours appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Exclusive eBook: Inside the stealthy startup that pitched brainless human clones
item.description: The ultimate plan to live forever is a brand new body. This subscriber-only eBook explores R3 Bio, a small startup that has pitched a startling and ethically charged vision for “brainless clones” to serve the role of backup human bodies. by Antonio Regalado March 20, 2026 Related...

item.title: PyTorch Lightning Compromised in PyPI Supply Chain Attack to Steal Credentials
item.description: In yet another software supply chain attack, threat actors have managed to compromise the popular Python package Lightning to push two malicious versions to conduct credential theft. According to Aikido Security, Socket, and StepSecurity, the two malicious versions are versions 2.6.2 and 2.6.3,...

item.title: This startup’s new mechanistic interpretability tool lets you debug LLMs
item.description: The San Francisco–based startup Goodfire just released a new tool, called Silico, that lets researchers and engineers peer inside an AI model and adjust its parameters—the settings that determine a model’s behavior—during training. This could give model makers more fine-grained control over how...

item.title: Anti-DDoS Firm Heaped Attacks on Brazilian ISPs
item.description: A Brazilian tech firm that specializes in protecting networks from distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) attacks has been enabling a botnet responsible for an extended campaign of massive DDoS attacks against other network operators in Brazil, KrebsOnSecurity has learned. The firm's chief executive...

item.title: ThreatsDay Bulletin: SMS Blaster Busts, OpenEMR Flaws, 600K Roblox Hacks and 25 More Stories
item.description: The internet is noisy this week. We are seeing some wild new tactics, like people using fake cell towers to send scam texts, while some developers are accidentally downloading tools that peek into their private files during a simple install. It is definitely a busy time to be online. Security is...

item.title: Dreame Launches 20+ Smart Home Products in San Francisco, Putting Its Robotic Arm Tech at the Center
item.description: At its DREAME NEXT event in San Francisco, Dreame Technology demonstrated how its bionic robotic arm technology has evolved from a robot vacuum feature into a cross-category platform powering everything from range hoods to dishwashers. The company also debuted the modular AURORA NEX smartphone and...

item.title: Possible Network Congestion in United States Eastern and Central regions
item.description: Apr 30, 13:30 UTCResolved - Cloudflare customers maybe be observing possible network congestion in the United States Eastern and Central regions, from 13:30 to 14:55 UTC.

item.title: Elevated errors on Claude Haiku 4.5
item.description: Apr 30, 13:10 UTCInvestigating - We are currently investigating this issue.

item.title: When "Big Female Lead" Dramas Run Out of Steam: The Trap and the Future of Female Characters in Chinese TV
item.description: Despite the flood of "female-centered" Chinese dramas, the women on screen have rarely felt more flat—trapped between hollow empowerment slogans and industrial formulas that recycle the same tired tropes. This essay cuts through the noise to argue that real progress won't come from louder...

item.title: Focus Session: Autonomous Systems Dependability in the era of AI: Design Challenges in Safety, Security, Reliability and Certification
item.description: The design of embedded safety-critical systems such as those used in next-generation automotive and autonomous platforms, is increasingly challenged by escalating system complexity, hardware-software heterogeneity, and the integration of intelligent, data-driven components. Ensuring dependability...

item.title: Increased HTTP 5xx Errors in South Korea region
item.description: Apr 30, 12:40 UTCResolved - Some Cloudflare customers in South Korea region may have observed an increased rate of 5xx errors from 12:40 to 13:00 UTC.

item.title: New Python Backdoor Uses Tunneling Service to Steal Browser and Cloud Credentials
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a stealthy Python-based backdoor framework called DEEP#DOOR that comes with capabilities to establish persistent access and harvest a wide range of sensitive information from compromised hosts. "The intrusion chain begins with execution of a batch...

item.title: Enabling a new model for healthcare with AI co-clinician
item.description: Researching the path to AI-augmented care and development of an AI co-clinician.

item.title: The Download: the North Pole’s future and humanoid data
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. Digging for clues about the North Pole’s past In the past, getting to the North Pole involved a treacherous trip through ice many meters thick. But...

item.title: EtherRAT Distribution Spoofing Administrative Tools via GitHub Facades
item.description: Intro A sophisticated, high-resilience malicious campaign was identified by Atos Threat Research Center (TRC) in March 2026. This operation specifically targets the high-privilege professional accounts of enterprise administrators, DevOps engineers, and security analysts by impersonating...

item.title: XtalPi Reports First Full-Year Profit, Holds USD 1 Billion Cash
item.description: XtalPi posted its first full-year net profit of RMB 258 million ($36 million) on revenue of RMB 800 million, powered by a major pharma deal and rapid growth in its automated lab business, with over RMB 7 billion in cash for future expansion.

item.title: Shuowei Optoelectronics Nears USD 14 Million Funding for Space Solar
item.description: Beijing-based Shuowei Optoelectronics is closing a nearly RMB 100 million ($14 million) funding round led by Qifu Capital to advance its perovskite-based solar wing technology for commercial space applications.

item.title: Linkhou Raises USD Tens of Millions to Expand Robotics Components Production
item.description: Suzhou-based Linkhou has raised hundreds of millions of yuan in a Series B+ round led by China Internet Investment Fund to scale production of its core components for embodied AI robots, including vision modules and mobile chassis.

item.title: Judge, Then Drive: A Critic-Centric Vision Language Action Framework for Autonomous Driving
item.description: Recent advances in vision language action (VLA) models have shown remarkable potential for autonomous driving by directly mapping multimodal inputs to control signals. However, previous VLA-based methods have not explicitly exploited the critic capability of VLAs to refine driving decisions, even...

item.title: claude.ai and API unavailable
item.description: Apr 30, 01:51 UTCResolved - This incident has been resolved.Apr 30, 01:34 UTCMonitoring - A fix has been implemented and we are monitoring the results.Apr 30, 01:20 UTCInvestigating - We are currently investigating this issue.

item.title: Pragmos: A Process Agentic Modeling System
item.description: The advent of Large Language Models (LLMs) has significantly transformed tasks across Software Engineering. In the context of Business Process Management, LLMs are now being explored as tools to derive process models directly from textual descriptions. Existing approaches range from chatbot-driven...
